title: How to Build Agentic RAG with Hybrid Search
summary:

The post How to Build Agentic RAG with Hybrid Search appeared first on Towards Data Science.

title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
summary: Peter Sarlin's startup QuTwo is building quantum computing infrastructure for enterprises. This matters because it prepares businesses for a future where quantum computing becomes a reality.

title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
summary: Truecaller introduces a new feature allowing one family member to block or end suspicious calls on behalf of others. This feature lets an admin receive alerts about potential scams and intervene to protect family members.

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
summary: Researchers introduce DIVE, a method to increase diversity in task synthesis for generalizable tool use in LLMs. DIVE achieves this by executing diverse tools first and then deriving tasks from the resulting traces.

title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
summary: Researchers identify a new bottleneck in autonomous driving: robust and generalizable reasoning. This deficit hinders systems in complex social interactions and long-tail scenarios, prompting a need for a cognitive engine.

title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
summary: Researchers introduce PACED, a framework that optimizes large language model (LLM) distillation by focusing on the student model's competence frontier. This approach reduces waste by concentrating on problems the student is struggling with, but not mastering.

title: Measuring AI Agents' Progress on Multi-Step Cyber Attack Scenarios
summary: Researchers evaluate AI models' autonomous cyber-attack capabilities on multi-step scenarios, observing performance scales log-linearly with compute budget and successive models outperform predecessors. This matters as it highlights AI's potential for rapid improvement and increased threat capabilities.

title: Reversible Lifelong Model Editing via Semantic Routing-Based LoRA
summary: Researchers propose SoLA, a framework for reversible lifelong model editing in Large Language Models. SoLA uses semantic routing-based LoRA modules to prevent semantic drift and catastrophic forgetting, allowing for precise updates.

title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
summary: Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang will deliver the keynote address at GTC 2026, focusing on the company's vision for computing and AI. This matters as it will unveil Nvidia's new products and strategies, setting the stage for the tech industry's future developments.

title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
summary: Rox AI, a sales automation startup, has reached a $1.2B valuation. It offers AI-native alternatives to traditional CRM tools.

title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
summary: US military may utilize generative AI to rank targets and provide strike recommendations, which would be reviewed by humans. This development matters as it raises concerns about AI-driven decision-making in military operations.

title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
summary: Facebook Marketplace now enables sellers to use Meta AI to auto-draft replies to buyer inquiries. This feature streamlines communication, saving sellers time and effort.

title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
summary: Tinder introduces in-person events and virtual speed dating to revive user base. This revamp aims to attract younger daters and reengage existing users.

title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
summary: Atlassian laid off 1,600 employees (10% of its workforce) to redirect funds to AI development. This move mirrors Block's recent staff cuts, prioritizing AI investment.

title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
summary: Bumble introduces AI-powered "Bee" to match users based on compatibility and goals. This new feature will shift the app's focus beyond swiping, enabling more personalized matches.

title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
summary: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for allegedly using her work without consent. This matters as it raises concerns about AI-powered tools exploiting authors' work without permission.

title: Exploratory Data Analysis for Credit Scoring with Python
summary:
Understanding default risk through statistical analysis of borrower and loan characteristics.

title: Solving the Human Training Data Problem
summary:

summary:

title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
summary: Product engineers increasingly use AI to enhance and streamline design in everyday products like cars, appliances, and medical devices. This matters because AI can improve safety, efficiency, and functionality in real-world applications.

title: I Finally Built My First AI App (And It Wasn’t What I Expected)
summary:
A beginner-friendly walkthrough of API calls, environment variables, and real-world AI infrastructure
The post I Finally Built My First AI App (And It Wasn’t What I Expected) appeared first on Towards Data Science.

title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
summary: Researchers propose a system to automate data product improvement using AI agents in a continuous optimization loop. This system enables domain experts to refine data products with human oversight, balancing automation and trust.

title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
summary: Researchers propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory for GUI agents that combines symbolic and continuous representations. This innovation enables GUI agents to learn from and adapt to complex workflows and interfaces.

title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
summary: Researchers propose Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning (HEAL), a framework to distill reasoning capabilities from large models into smaller ones. HEAL addresses the "Teacher Ceiling" limitation by actively intervening in the learning process.

title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
summary: Researchers introduce TRACED, a framework to assess LLM reasoning quality through geometric kinematics, decomposing reasoning into Progress and Stability. This approach improves performance and robustness across benchmarks by identifying correct reasoning patterns.

title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
summary: Researchers propose new prompt-based uncertainty elicitation techniques for large language models using imprecise probabilities. This addresses limitations in classical probabilistic uncertainty frameworks, particularly in ambiguous question-answering and self-reflection settings.

title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
Autonomous context compression
summary:
New: A tool for autonomous context compression has been added to the Deep Agents SDK and CLI, enabling models to compress their own context windows. 
This matters because it allows for more efficient use of working memory in AI models, potentially improving performance and reducing computational costs.

title: An Intuitive Guide to MCMC (Part I): The Metropolis-Hastings Algorithm
summary:
Tired of the AI hype? Let's talk about the probabilistic algorithms actually driving high-end quantitative finance.
The post An Intuitive Guide to MCMC (Part I): The Metropolis-Hastings Algorithm appeared first on Towards Data Science.

title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
summary: Rakuten now uses Codex from OpenAI to automate software development, cutting MTTR by 50% and delivering full-stack builds in weeks. This matters because it significantly speeds up software deployment and reduces errors.

title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
summary: China's OpenClaw AI tool is gaining popularity, with users creating automated scripts to complete tasks. This surge in adoption is attracting entrepreneurs who see profit opportunities in the tool's capabilities.

title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
summary: Researchers design AI agents to resist "prompt injection" attacks by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data. This matters as it enhances AI security against social engineering and data breaches.

title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
summary: OpenAI has developed an agent runtime using its Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers. This enables secure, scalable agents to run with files, tools, and state, advancing AI capabilities.

title: Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI
summary: Wayfair leverages OpenAI models to automate ticket triage and enhance product attributes. This matters as it boosts support speed and catalog accuracy at scale.

title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
summary:
New: Researchers define an "agent harness" as the system engineering around AI models to turn intelligence into useful work. 
This matters because harness engineering is crucial for making AI models practical and effective in real-world applications.

title: How Coding Agents Are Reshaping Engineering, Product and Design
summary: Coding agents are emerging as a new approach to EPD, automating tasks and streamlining software development. This shift matters because it can significantly improve efficiency and productivity in software creation.

title: Building a strong data infrastructure for AI agent success
summary: Companies are rapidly deploying AI agents, with 88% using AI in at least one function by 2025. This growth highlights the increasing importance of robust data infrastructure for AI success.

title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
summary: Pokémon Go is being used to train delivery robots' navigation systems by providing them with precise, real-world location data. This collaboration matters as it could significantly improve the efficiency and safety of autonomous delivery robots.

title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
summary: Researchers developed IH-Challenge, a training method for frontier LLMs to prioritize trusted instructions. This improves instruction hierarchy, safety, steerability, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

title: Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub
Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries

How we built LangChain’s GTM Agent
summary: LangChain developed a GTM (Go-To-Market) agent that boosts lead conversion by 250% and frees up 40 hours/month per sales rep. It matters because this AI-powered sales assistant can significantly improve sales efficiency and conversion rates.

title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
summary: AI-powered conflict tracking dashboards are being shared online, allowing users to monitor the Iran conflict in real-time. This matters because it reveals the increasing accessibility and transparency of military intelligence, potentially influencing public perception and global understanding of the conflict.

title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform, secured $100 million in funding to challenge AWS with AI-native infrastructure. This investment values Railway as a significant infrastructure startup capitalizing on developer frustration with traditional platforms.

title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
summary: Claude Code's AI coding tool now costs up to $200/month, sparking a rebellion among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative, offers identical functionality, running on local machines without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
summary: Listen Labs secured $69 million in funding after a viral billboard campaign that drew thousands of developers with a coding challenge. This unconventional approach led to the hiring of 430 engineers, some of whom got hired by the company.

title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
summary: Salesforce has released a rebuilt Slackbot AI agent with advanced capabilities, including data search, document drafting, and automated actions. This move positions Slack at the center of the emerging "agentic AI" movement, countering Microsoft and Google's workplace AI efforts.

title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
summary: Anthropic released Cowork, a non-technical AI agent that integrates with files, built in under 2 weeks using its own Claude Code tool. This marks a major inflection point in mainstream AI adoption, positioning Anthropic to compete with Microsoft's Copilot in AI-powered productivity tools.
